mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 3.72% combined with CPI at 326.030 signals an ongoing monetary tightening regime. Sustained elevated FEDFUNDS suppresses equity valuations and increases real discount rates, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the market reprices growth expectations downward over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with persistent inflation
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yield declines over 20 trading days despite FEDFUNDS remaining above 3.5%, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.4% combined with payroll employment at 159,526 thousand indicates a tight labor market. Persistent labor tightness signals wage pressure and inflation persistence, forcing the Fed to maintain or raise rates, which increases Treasury yields as the market extends the tightening cycle horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.5% AND PAYEMS growth exceeds 100k per month
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor market
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields fall by more than 10 basis points over 20 trading days when both UNRATE < 4.5% and PAYEMS growth > 100k, or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in rolling regressions.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) at 735,904 million and housing starts (HOUST) at 1,246 thousand indicate sustained consumer and residential demand. Strong demand signals inflationary pressure and reduces the probability of near-term rate cuts, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as the terminal rate expectation shifts higher.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS month-over-month growth > 0.5% AND HOUST > 1,200 thousand
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields decline over 20 trading days when both demand signals are strong, or the joint effect of RSAFS and HOUST explains less than 5% of yield variance in rolling 60-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 101.7935 (index 2017=100) shows production above trend. Elevated supply-side activity combined with tight labor markets creates stagflationary pressure. The market reprices inflation expectations upward, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as real yields compress and nominal yields rise.
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields fall by more than 5 basis points over 5 trading days when INDPRO > 101.5 and growing, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 6-month window.

mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 3.72% is above the estimated neutral rate. If FEDFUNDS rises above 4.0% in the next month, the Fed enters restrictive territory, signaling extended tightening. This triggers a repricing of long-duration assets, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as growth expectations decline and recession risk rises.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 4.0% within 30 calendar days
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise by more than 15 basis points over 20 trading days following a FEDFUNDS rise above 4.0%, or the negative correlation between FEDFUNDS increases and yield changes reverses (becomes positive) in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) at 24,024.957 billion (chained 2017 dollars) combined with declining housing starts and retail sales signals demand deceleration. Weakening demand reduces inflation expectations and increases the probability of Fed rate cuts, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as the market reprices the terminal rate lower.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines > 5% quarter-over-quarter AND RSAFS growth < 0.2% month-over-month
regime_hint: demand-driven recession risk or growth slowdown
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise over 60 trading days when both HOUST and RSAFS show weakness, or the joint predictive power of demand indicators for yield direction falls below 8% R-squared in rolling 90-day windows.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 4.4% is near historical lows. If UNRATE rises above 4.8% within 60 days, it signals labor market deterioration and recession risk. The market reprices growth expectations downward and reduces terminal rate expectations, causing 10-year Treasury yields to fall as safe-haven demand increases.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.8% within 60 calendar days
regime_hint: labor market deterioration and recession onset
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields rise by more than 20 basis points over 60 trading days following a UNRATE rise above 4.8%, or the negative correlation between UNRATE increases and yield changes is not statistically significant (p > 0.15) in rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 101.7935 combined with elevated CPI at 326.030 indicates supply-side constraints and inflation persistence. If INDPRO declines below 101.0 while CPIAUCSL remains elevated, it signals stagflation. The market reprices inflation risk upward and growth risk downward, causing 10-year Treasury yields to rise as inflation premium dominates.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below 101.0 AND CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth > 0.3%
failure_condition: 10-year Treasury yields fall by more than 25 basis points over 60 trading days when stagflation signals are present, or the interaction effect between INDPRO decline and CPI elevation explains less than 6% of yield variance in rolling 120-day windows.
